arguments.task: Visit Stripe's official documentation and pricing pages. Read the Stripe API reference, authentication documentation, rate limits documentation, pricing page, and any official page about MCP or model context protocol if available. Return concise extracted findings with the exact official URLs visited for: one-line product/API description, all documented API auth methods, whether access is self-serve free/trial/paid/admin/partner gated, API surface type and breadth, rate limit notes, and whether Stripe has an official MCP server or docs mentioning MCP.

raw_result.description: Stripe provides APIs for online payment processing and broader internet commerce operations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Stripe's API docs and keys docs show API authentication via API keys, including secret keys, restricted API keys, organization API keys, managed API keys, and publishable keys; these are token-based credentials. The gathered evidence did not verify OAuth2, Basic, or API-key-in-query auth for the core API.
raw_result.gating_reason: Stripe provides self-serve developer access and pay-as-you-go pricing with no setup or monthly fees for standard usage.

api_surface.breadth: Comprehensive REST API reference covering core payments plus broader commerce resources, with separate v1 and v2 API references and many product areas.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Stripe has an official Model Context Protocol server documented by Stripe and marked public preview.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Stripe documents a basic live-mode rate limit of 100 operations per second and sandbox limit of 25 operations per second, with 429 responses and a Stripe-Rate-Limited-Reason header; some endpoints have lower per-endpoint limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official SDKs and CLI are available, the docs include dedicated authentication and rate-limit pages, and Stripe documents an official MCP server in public preview.
